title: messages: PeerDeliver.body and friends have no length cap; hostile peer causes unbounded alloc
description: ClearMessage::PeerDeliver, PeerTyping, PeerGroupUpdate, PeerRetract, PeerReceipt all contain plain String fields (body, chat_id, message_id, space_id, sent_at, at, etc.) with no length bound enforced at deserialization. A hostile MLS peer can send arbitrarily large strings. These are deserialized from MLS-decrypted bytes before any application-level check. Each field needs a custom deserializer with a reasonable cap (e.g. 1 MiB for body, 256 bytes for IDs/timestamps). The MLS 10 MiB chunk cap on FileChunk exists as a precedent.
